query: I'm still in early exploration on a tabular classification problem. I'm comparing logistic regression, random forest, and gradient boosting to see which is most promising. Should I package the model now?
expected_behavior: Does NOT engage the full packaging workflow (joblib artifact + manifest + smoke test + schema) for an exploratory notebook with no near-term deployment
Explains that packaging is a hand-off discipline for the chosen model just before deployment, not a step in model selection / exploration

Recommends a model-selection or evaluation workflow instead — e.g. building baseline comparisons (building-baseline-models) and / or fair evaluation (evaluating-binary-classifiers) — and suggests revisiting packaging once a single model is chosen for deployment
